design_philosophy.core_loop: Drop falling columns of three jewels, rotate/position them to create matches of three or more identical symbols horizontally, vertically, or diagonally; cleared jewels trigger gravity cascades for chain reactions
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Competitive stacking pressure—opponent's well filling forces victory conditions while managing your own space through point-spending abilities
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Strategic puzzle mastery in head-to-head competition; outwitting AI or human opponents through tactical gem placement and cascading chain reactions
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately competitive design with asymmetric resources (C-button spend mechanic to shrink opponent's space); integrates single-use consumable items (Barrier, Heavy Weight, Magic Gem, Hourglass) earned in story mode, creating distinct single-player vs. multiplayer resource economies

difficulty.continues_system: Hourglass consumables function as continues, allowing rematch if opponent wins twice; obtained in single-player mode
difficulty.checkpointing: Story mode structured as progressive pyramid rooms with incrementally difficult themed opponents (bats, mummies); three difficulty tiers (easy, medium, hard) determine accessible level depth
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects difficulty tier at mode start; difficulty directly gates progression depth (easy/medium end game early vs. hard unlocks full pyramid)

progression.upgrade_system: Single-use consumable items collected during story progression: Barrier, Heavy Weight, Magic Gem, Hourglass. Magic Jewels (multicolor, appear occasionally) provide on-board effects (shrink opponent, unshrink player board, mass color-based destruction)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story mode unlocks progressively deeper pyramid rooms based on opponent victories; difficulty tier determines how many rooms/levels unlock; special Magic Jewel columns appear dynamically during play
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty tiers with distinct progression depth
Competitive multiplayer mode supports indefinite rematches
Consumable item inventory management adds replay planning layer
Different AI opponent themes (bats, mummies) across pyramid rooms

world_building_techniques: Pyramid room setting establishes Egyptian theme
Themed AI opponents (bats, mummies) reinforce location atmosphere
Progressive room navigation implies story arc through pyramid exploration

boss_design.philosophy: AI opponents serve as difficulty gates within pyramid progression; escalating challenge via opponent competence rather than mechanical uniqueness

faction_indicators: Themed opponent variety (bats vs. mummies) suggests environmental faction differentiation
danger_signals: Opponent well-filling mechanic creates direct threat visualization
Magic Jewel disruptions introduce unpredictable board hazards
Accelerating fall speed increases perceived time pressure
environmental_storytelling: Pyramid room progression from entrance to depths
Themed enemy encounters (bats=early chambers, mummies=deeper passages)
Egyptian aesthetic through visual setting and opponent design
